title: Traits 5
description: Trait objects (`dyn Trait`) allow working with different concrete types through a single interface at runtime.
instructions: Use trait objects to store different types in a single collection.
hints: Use `Box<dyn Trait>` to create trait objects that can hold different concrete types.
The Vec type should be `Vec<Box<dyn Draw>>`.
Each element is boxed to put it on the heap with a known size.
explanation: Trait objects (`dyn Trait`) enable dynamic dispatch - the method to call is determined at runtime. `Box<dyn Draw>` is a pointer to any type implementing Draw, with a vtable for method lookup. This is Rust's approach to polymorphism. Unlike generics (static dispatch), trait objects have a small runtime cost but allow heterogeneous collections.
